core_model.structure_summary: A vector state is iteratively mapped by comparing it against a stored set of reference vectors through a similarity operator, passing the scores through a nonlinear separation function, and projecting back, with the fixed points characterized as minima of an associated scalar energy functional.
core_model.canonical_form: x^{t+1} = P\,\mathrm{sep}\big(\mathrm{sim}(M,x^t)\big);\qquad \tau\,\dot x = -\nabla_x E(x),\quad E(x)=\tfrac{1}{2}\lVert x\rVert^2 - \log\!\sum_j e^{\,\mathrm{sim}(M_j,x)}

variables.meaning: vector iterated toward a stored pattern
continuous relaxation parameter in the dynamical form
scalar energy functional whose minima are the fixed points

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order gradient-flow relaxation of a state toward minima of a scalar energy functional built from a self-quadratic term minus a log-sum-exp of similarity scores against fixed reference vectors.

notes: Two equivalent framings appear: a discrete similarity-separation-projection update and its continuous gradient-flow ODE with a Lyapunov energy. I took the general update rule as the core object; the skeleton is the gradient flow / stationarity condition of that energy. The separation function (softmax/log-sum-exp) supplies the normalization and multiplicative-update character.
